brief.key_message: Our new pediatric oncology imaging service brings hope and care to children and their families.
open_questions: What is the official name of the new service?
Are there any specific branding guidelines or logos to include?
Is there a tagline or call to action preferred?
What are the exact dimensions for the banner?
Should any contact information or referral instructions be included?
Is there a launch date or event to reference?
risks: Risk of appearing insensitive if the celebratory tone is not balanced with the seriousness of pediatric oncology.
Potential for misrepresentation if actual patient images are used without consent.
Overly generic imagery may not resonate with medical professionals.

retrieved_rules.title: Healthcare imagery
retrieved_rules.text:
Do not depict patients in distress, invasive procedures, blood, needles, or
operating-theatre scenes. Do not stage children, families, or patients in
ways that read as marketing emotion-mining. When in doubt, use abstract or
data-network imagery over patient photography.

retrieved_rules.title: Healthcare audiences (M42 specifically)
retrieved_rules.text:
For M42 and any healthcare-adjacent audience, language must be evidence-aware,
non-sensational, and trust-building. Do not use battle metaphors (fight, defeat,
beat the disease) in patient-facing or clinical-facing communications. Avoid
unfounded efficacy claims. Use professional, dignified language even in
emotionally weighty contexts (pediatrics, oncology, end-of-life).

retrieved_rules.title: OpCo voice notes
retrieved_rules.text:
- **G42 (master):** strategic, sovereign-AI-positioned, geo-political-aware tone.
- **M42:** healthcare-precise, evidence-aware, patient-respectful.
- **Core42:** enterprise-cloud, sovereign-cloud, regulated-industry-aware.
- **Inception:** AI-foundational-models, builder-and-researcher-facing tone.
- **Mubadala Health:** clinical-precision, patient-experience-led, regional pride.

copy.headline: Advanced pediatric oncology imaging, designed for clarity
copy.subhead: Evidence-informed imaging to support timely, confident decisions in complex pediatric oncology cases.
copy.body: Introduce your patients to a dedicated pediatric oncology imaging pathway that prioritizes diagnostic precision, coordinated care, and a respectful family experience.

visual_spec.concept: Abstract, softly colorful data and imaging-inspired forms subtly suggesting a child’s silhouette, conveying precision, care, and optimism without showing real patients.
visual_spec.composition: Clean banner with abstract imaging motif as hero background, headline and subhead right-aligned on light overlay, logo and CTA anchored in a clear lower corner with ample whitespace.
must_include: Relevant healthcare brand logo (per final brand guidance)
Clear, legible typography suitable for clinical audiences
Abstract imaging or data-network graphics suggesting pediatric focus without depicting identifiable patients
must_avoid: Images of children or families in distress or overt emotional poses
Any depiction of invasive procedures, blood, needles, or operating theatres
Overly playful or cartoonish visuals that trivialize oncology
Sensational or battle-themed language in on-image copy

final_draft.rationale: Copy emphasizes diagnostic precision, coordinated care, and a respectful family experience to align with evidence-aware, trust-building language expectations for healthcare audiences (Rule 2, Rule 10), while avoiding battle metaphors or overstated efficacy. Visual direction uses abstract imaging and data motifs instead of real patient photography to mitigate sensitivity risks in pediatric oncology and comply with restrictions on distressing or invasive medical imagery (Rule 5). Warm but professional mood and a neutral CTA are chosen to balance the brief’s uplifting intent with the seriousness of pediatric oncology and the clinical focus of referring physicians; assumptions about service name, specific logos, and referral mechanics are left generic pending clarification (Rule 2, Rule 10).

reasoning.brand_voice: The copy is evidence-aware, professional, and avoids forbidden metaphors or sensational claims, aligning with Rule 2 and Rule 10.
reasoning.visual_spec: The visual concept uses abstract, non-distressing imagery and avoids all forbidden elements per Rule 5, with a clean, clinical composition.
reasoning.audience_fit: Register is professional and trust-building, CTA is neutral and appropriate for referring physicians, fully matching Rule 2 and Rule 10.

delivery.message: Draft passed brand rubric. Ready to deliver.

output.query_text: Asset type: banner | Audience: referring physicians | OpCo: None | Tone hints: uplifting, joyful, celebratory, warm, eye-catching | Key message: Our new pediatric oncology imaging service brings hope and care to children and their families.
